title: AutoTheoSpec
description: Output of the Researcher subagent. Input to the Modeler subagent.

family_signature.description: Format: <family>:<subtype>:<resolution_pattern>. Two events with the same signature share a pipeline. Examples: 'truflation:eggs:daily_threshold', 'sports:nba:total_points', 'fed:rate_decision:meeting_outcome'.

rule_quote_verbatim.description: Exact text from Kalshi market description. Forces auditability.

threshold_per_strike.description: How to extract the threshold from each market ticker. Usually 'parsed from market ticker last segment after T'.

blackout_rules.description: Human-readable rules the pipeline should encode. E.g. 'T-15min to T+30min around Truflation publish'.

backtest.description: Backtester directives. Optional. Used when the historical archive does not faithfully represent the live underlying (e.g. forward-fill of a coarser-frequency real source) — in which case the calibration gate is structurally biased and would always fail. Setting archive_quality != 'real' tells the harness to skip the calibration-decile gate but still run leakage and refusal-sanity checks; ship-with-trip is enforced by trip_max_loss_usd_override (typically $5).

archive_quality.description: Real = actual point-in-time observations of the underlying. Synthetic_forward_fill = derived from a coarser-frequency real source (e.g. weekly forward-filled to daily). Synthetic_implied_only = derived from market resolutions (e.g. Kalshi implied bounds). None = no historical archive available.

skip_calibration_gate_reason.description: Required when archive_quality != 'real'. Free text explaining why calibration cannot be validated against this archive.

trip_max_loss_usd_override.description: When archive_quality != 'real', override the default $25/24h trip with a tighter cap (typically $5).
